title: tests.run como herramienta MIASI controlada
wave: Ola 3 — Seguridad operacional, aprobación humana y ejecución controlada

architectural_decision.reason: FUNC-SPRINT-32 compone capacidades ya aprobadas: Approval Workflow, PolicyEngine, MIASI, SafeSubprocessRunner, ReportEngine y EventLogger. No agrega dependencias externas, red, UI, backend nuevo ni ejecución arbitraria. Es una evolución interna y restringida del modelo de seguridad operacional.

pass_criteria: tests.run appears in MIASI as implemented-initial.
tests.run executes only configured pytest profiles.
tests.run requires scoped approval when PolicyEngine requires it.
No shell=True or arbitrary command execution is introduced.
Exit code, stdout, stderr, timeout and redactions are captured.
Optional JSON/Markdown reports and local events are produced.
MIASI structural schema allows implemented-initial status for executable tool registry entries.
block_criteria: tests.run executes without valid approval when approval is required.
Secrets are printed raw in reports or traces.
The sprint claims CI/CD, sandbox, patch apply, refactor execution, Git write or deploy support.
risks: Pytest can execute local test code, so tests.run remains approval-gated.
The implementation is not a full filesystem sandbox.
The initial profile registry is intentionally narrow and must be governed before expansion.
Redaction is a conservative first line and will be hardened in Sprint 33.
next_sprint: FUNC-SPRINT-33 — Hardening de SecretGuard y checks básicos de prompt/tool injection
